Title: Metabolic variations in root tissues and rhizosphere soils of weak host plants potently lead to distinct host status and chemotaxis regulation of Meloidogyne incognita in intercropping

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CsPTS promoter (~2000 bp) for promoter:GUS; constitutive overexpression construct in pCAMBIA1305 (promoter not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Pantothenate and CoA biosynthesis; beta-alanine metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream/terminal synthesis step for pantothenic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root tissues, especially amaranth root tissues under Meloidogyne incognita infection; studied via cucumber hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with plant metabolic defense and delayed nematode development in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression in cucumber hairy roots delayed Meloidogyne incognita development and reduced root gall formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsPTS was identified as the cucumber homolog involved in pantothenic acid biosynthesis corresponding to amaranth-enriched differential metabolites. 2. CsPTS expression was significantly up-regulated at 21 DAI in infected cucumber roots and root galls. 3. CsPTS promoter activity was concentrated in vascular bundles, lateral root junctions, and gall regions. 4. CsPTS overexpression in cucumber hairy roots delayed nematode development and reduced gall numbers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: KEGG-based homolog screening; qRT-PCR; promoter:GUS hairy root assay; transgenic hairy root overexpression; fuchsin staining and developmental-stage scoring; gall counting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Meloidogyne incognita infection; hairy root system assessed mainly at 14-21 DAI
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in infected cucumber roots and root galls, strongest at 21 DAI; promoter active in vascular bundles, primary lateral root junctions, and gall areas
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted metabolomics of root tissues and rhizosphere soils; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate metabolic engineering target to enhance root-associated resistance to root-knot nematode by increasing pantothenic acid biosynthesis; may be used in hairy-root or whole-plant engineering, potentially combined with other defensive metabolite pathways
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not reported for CsPTS overexpression alone
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causal metabolite evidence is based on homolog selection from cucumber to mimic amaranth-enriched metabolic pathways rather than direct quantification of pantothenic acid after CsPTS overexpression.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CsCHS promoter (~2000 bp) for promoter:GUS; constitutive overexpression construct in pCAMBIA1305 (promoter not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream entry step to flavonoid/chalcone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Chalcone intermediate leading to phloretin-related metabolites
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root tissues, especially amaranth root tissues under Meloidogyne incognita infection; studied via cucumber hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated phenolic/flavonoid metabolite linked to delayed nematode development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression in cucumber hairy roots delayed Meloidogyne incognita development and reduced root gall formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCHS was identified as the cucumber homolog associated with biosynthesis of phloretin corresponding to amaranth-enriched differential metabolites. 2. CsCHS expression was significantly up-regulated at 21 DAI in infected cucumber roots and root galls. 3. CsCHS promoter activity localized to vascular bundles, lateral root junctions, and gall regions. 4. CsCHS overexpression in cucumber hairy roots delayed nematode development and reduced gall numbers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: KEGG-based homolog screening; qRT-PCR; promoter:GUS hairy root assay; transgenic hairy root overexpression; fuchsin staining and developmental-stage scoring; gall counting
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry enzyme of flavonoid biosynthesis, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Meloidogyne incognita infection; hairy root system assessed mainly at 14-21 DAI
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in infected cucumber roots and root galls, strongest at 21 DAI; promoter active in vascular bundles, primary lateral root junctions, and gall areas
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase, chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted metabolomics of root tissues and rhizosphere soils; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target to strengthen flavonoid-related root defense against root-knot nematodes; could be stacked with CsPTS or metabolite-based strategies
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not reported for CsCHS overexpression alone
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper links CsCHS to phloretin biosynthesis through homolog/pathway inference from amaranth-enriched metabolites rather than direct enzymatic assay of phloretin synthesis in cucumber.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Neuropeptide-mediated regulation of chemotaxis and infection behavior; expression is repressed by amaranth root exudates and by naringenin/salicin
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chemotaxis toward host roots and parasitic infection behavior affecting access to root-derived nutrients
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Repel/inhibit Meloidogyne incognita, suppress egg hatching and root infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Meloidogyne incognita chemotaxis toward cucumber and supports infection; reduced expression decreases attraction, delays development, and lowers galling/egg masses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pivotal positive regulator of nematode chemotaxis toward cucumber; strongest RNAi phenotype among tested flp genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Behavioral chemotaxis and infection programs in M. incognita
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cucumber versus amaranth root exudates; intercropping context; naringenin; salicin
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cucumber-amaranth intercropping; root exudate treatment; metabolite treatment with naringenin or salicin; in vitro RNAi
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in infective J2s than pre-infective J2s; reduced in nematodes from intercropping side and after amaranth exudate treatment; low in J4/females
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in infective J2s; low in J4/females
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted metabolomics of plant/root/rhizosphere context; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR across life stages and treatments; in vitro dsRNA soaking RNAi; one-choice attraction assay; cucumber infection assay; developmental staging; egg mass and gall quantification
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for nematode control via host-delivered RNAi or metabolite-mediated suppression; compatible with intercropping or rhizosphere metabolite deployment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mi-flp-1 expression was reduced in nematodes under cucumber-amaranth intercropping and after treatment with amaranth root exudates. 2. RNAi of Mi-flp-1 significantly decreased chemotaxis toward cucumber root tips. 3. Mi-flp-1 silencing reduced nematode numbers, egg masses, and root galls and delayed development in cucumber roots. 4. Naringenin and salicin lowered Mi-flp-1 expression, linking amaranth rhizosphere metabolites to disrupted nematode chemotaxis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although classified here as a regulatory gene because it governs behavior, it is not a plant transcription factor; it is a nematode neuropeptide gene controlling host seeking.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Neuropeptide-mediated regulation of chemotaxis and infection behavior; expression is repressed by amaranth root exudates and by naringenin/salicin
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chemotaxis toward host roots and parasitic infection behavior affecting access to root-derived nutrients
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Repel/inhibit Meloidogyne incognita, suppress egg hatching and root infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Meloidogyne incognita infection and host-seeking; reduced expression decreases infection, delays development, and lowers galling/egg masses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pivotal regulator of nematode chemotaxis/infection together with Mi-flp-1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Behavioral chemotaxis and infection programs in M. incognita
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cucumber versus amaranth root exudates; intercropping context; naringenin; salicin
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cucumber-amaranth intercropping; root exudate treatment; metabolite treatment with naringenin or salicin; in vitro RNAi
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in nematodes from intercropping side and after amaranth exudate treatment; low in J4/females
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low in J4/females; detailed early-stage enrichment not emphasized compared with flp-1/12/14/16
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted metabolomics of plant/root/rhizosphere context; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR across treatments; in vitro dsRNA soaking RNAi; cucumber infection assay; developmental staging; egg mass and gall quantification
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for RNAi-based nematode management or for metabolite-guided suppression in intercropping systems
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mi-flp-18 expression was decreased in nematodes under intercropping and after amaranth root exudate treatment. 2. RNAi of Mi-flp-18 reduced nematode abundance, galling, and egg masses in cucumber roots. 3. Mi-flp-18 silencing delayed nematode development in cucumber. 4. Naringenin and salicin lowered Mi-flp-18 expression, supporting a metabolite-mediated disruption of chemotaxis and infection.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The attraction assay strongest direct chemotaxis reduction was highlighted for Mi-flp-1, but Mi-flp-18 also showed a strong infection-related phenotype.
